description: Figure 3 presents a 3D surface plot illustrating the variation of the stress softening coefficient $k_s(\varepsilon+\Delta\varepsilon)$ as a function of the Zener-Hollomon parameters for two consecutive deformation stages and the dynamic recrystallization (DRX) fraction in low-alloy steel. The horizontal axes represent $\ln Z_P^{(I)}$ (first stage) and $\ln Z_P^{(II)}(\varepsilon+\Delta\varepsilon)$ (second stage), both ranging approximately from 22 to 36, while the vertical axis displays the first-stage DRX fraction $\varphi_d^{(I)}$ spanning 0 to 1 across four horizontal planes. The color scale indicates $k_s$ values from 0.2 (dark blue, representing maximum softening) to 1.0 (yellow, representing no softening). The visualization reveals that $k_s$ declines with increasing difference between $Z_P$ of the two stages and rising $\varphi_d^{(I)}$, as exhibited by the transition from yellow to blue regions at high $\varphi_d^{(I)}$ and large differences between the logarithmic Zener-Hollomon parameters. This demonstrates that prior deformation history and transient variations in deformation conditions significantly influence the saturation stress softening behavior during multi-stage thermomechanical processing.
